display.purpose: Product title only; avoid hero-scale typography.

title.purpose: Subsections such as logs and environment output groups.

body.purpose: Tables, forms, operational copy, and values.

label.purpose: Form labels and compact control text.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Future state transitions for hover, focus, disabled, and htmx loading feedback.

components.name: Default Button
components.description: Compact action button for safe operations such as add, save, deploy, retry, and drift detection.
components.css: .ds-btn-default { font: 400 1rem system-ui, sans-serif; color: #111827; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #d8dee7; border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 12px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease-out, border-color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-default:hover { background: #eef2f6; border-color: #255f85; color: #174565; } .ds-btn-default:active { background: #e2e8f0; } .ds-btn-default:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #255f85; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-default:disabled { opacity: .5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Danger Button
components.description: Destructive action button for delete, destroy, and confirm destroy.
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { font: 400 1rem system-ui, sans-serif; color: #b42318; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #b42318; border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 12px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease-out, color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-danger:hover { color: #ffffff; background: #b42318; } .ds-btn-danger:active { background: #8b1a12; border-color: #8b1a12; } .ds-btn-danger:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #b42318; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Compact text field for account aliases, project names, regions, and environment names.
components.css: .ds-field { display: block; font: 400 1rem system-ui, sans-serif; color: #111827; } .ds-field span { display: block; margin: 0 0 4px; } .ds-input { font: inherit; color: #111827; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #d8dee7; border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 12px; min-width: 14rem; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #4b5563; opacity: 1; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #255f85; outline-offset: 2px; border-color: #255f85; }

components.name: Status Text
components.description: Semantic status label for environment and job states.
components.html:
Ready: environment is up

Failed: retry or destroy

Destroyed
components.css: .ds-status { margin: 0 0 8px; font: 400 1rem system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-status-success { color: #087443; } .ds-status-danger { color: #b42318; } .ds-status-muted { color: #4b5563; }

components.name: Data Table
components.description: Compact full-width table for accounts, projects, blueprints, and environments.
components.css: .ds-table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; font: 400 1rem system-ui, sans-serif; color: #111827; } .ds-table th, .ds-table td { border: 1px solid #d8dee7; padding: 8px 12px; text-align: left; vertical-align: top; } .ds-table th { font-weight: 700; color: #4b5563; background: #eef2f6; }

components.name: Top Navigation
components.description: Simple horizontal navigation across account, project, blueprint, and environment work areas.
components.html: AccountsProjectsBlueprintsEnvironments
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; align-items: center; font: 400 1rem system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-nav-link { min-height: 36px; border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 12px; color: #4b5563; text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #111827; background: #eef2f6; } .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #255f85; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-nav-link-active { color: #ffffff; background: #255f85; font-weight: 700; }

components.name: Log Panel
components.description: Scrollable live output panel for provisioning and destroy logs.
components.css: .ds-log { box-sizing: border-box; max-height: 420px; overflow: auto; margin: 0; background: #101828; color: #e5e7eb; border-radius: 6px; padding: 16px; font: 400 0.875rem ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, monospace; line-height: 1.5; white-space: pre-wrap; } .ds-log:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #e5e7eb; outline-offset: 2px; }

narrative.overview:
Hermes should feel like a compact operations console for real AWS resources: quiet, legible, and direct. The implementation remains deliberately lightweight - Go templates, htmx, server-rendered tables and forms, and a Tailwind-compiled stylesheet - so the design system strengthens the product shape without replacing it with a heavy application framework.

The visual direction is restrained product UI. It preserves fast scanning for accounts, projects, blueprints, environments, previews, and logs. Tailwind provides consistent primitives, clearer hierarchy, focus states, and safer destructive-action affordances while keeping the page simple enough to remain a self-hosted tool.
keyCharacteristics: Compact server-rendered workflows with readable forms and tables.
Restrained neutrals with semantic status colors reserved for real state.
System typography only; no display fonts, no decorative type pairing.
Flat by default, with borders and tonal contrast doing the work.
Explicit action hierarchy for preview, provision, retry, drift detection, and destroy.

rules.name: The Semantic Scarcity Rule
rules.body: Red and green are reserved for actual state, never for decoration or brand flourish.

rules.name: The One Accent Future Rule
rules.body: When Tailwind is introduced, add one restrained primary accent for primary actions and active navigation; keep it under 10% of any screen.

rules.name: The Native Tool Rule
rules.body: Use one system font stack across UI labels, tables, buttons, and forms. No display font belongs in the console.

rules.name: The Fixed Scale Rule
rules.body: Product UI type sizes are fixed rem values, not viewport-scaled hero type.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Use borders, spacing, and tonal layers before shadows.

rules.name: The Log Contrast Rule
rules.body: The log panel may use a strong dark surface because it is a distinct stream of machine output; do not apply that dark treatment broadly to the whole app.

dos: Do preserve the self-hosted, lightweight feel: Go templates, htmx flows, and server-rendered forms should remain first-class.
Do use restrained neutral surfaces, clear dividers, and one future primary accent for primary actions and active navigation.
Do make risky actions explicit: preview, confirm create, destroy preview, confirm destroy, retry, and drift detection need distinct copy and visual hierarchy.
Do give every interactive primitive hover, active, disabled, and visible focus states.
Do keep empty states useful: point users toward adding an account, creating a project, saving a blueprint, or deploying an environment.
Do keep log output visually distinct with the dark log surface and a readable text color.
donts: Don't use marketing-style SaaS pages, oversized hero sections, decorative gradients, playful illustrations, or ornamental dashboards.
Don't use terminal-only aesthetics that make normal forms and tables feel hostile.
Don't copy AWS-console-level density where common actions become hard to scan.
Don't make destructive actions look identical to safe actions.
Don't rely on red or green for decoration; they are semantic state colors only.
Don't introduce custom scrollbars, unusual form controls, or modal-heavy flows for flavor.
